parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: Your class has attended a seminar about how adults could be encouraged to keep learning throughout their lives. You have made the notes below. Write an essay discussing two of the ideas in your notes. You should explain which idea would be more effective, giving reasons to support your opinion.

stimulus.title: How could adults be encouraged to keep learning throughout their lives?
stimulus.body:
• requiring employers to fund training time for staff
• offering free evening classes in community centres
• recognising short online courses with official qualifications

Some opinions expressed in the seminar:
"Nobody studies after a ten-hour shift unless the boss gives them the hours."
"The classes exist - people just never hear about them."
"A certificate no employer recognises is just a nice email."

bullets: explain which would be more effective
give reasons to support your opinion

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in 220-260 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: An English-language education website has invited reviews of online courses.

stimulus.title: Online course reviews wanted
stimulus.body: Review an online course you have completed. Describe what the course promised and how it was taught, evaluate whether it delivered real skills rather than just videos to watch, and say what kind of learner it would suit best.

bullets: what the course promised and how it was taught
what kind of learner it would suit

tasks.lead: Your town council wants to promote adult learning and has invited proposals for a weekend learning festival.

stimulus.title: Proposal: a community learning festival
stimulus.body: Write a proposal for a weekend festival at which local people teach each other skills. Describe how the festival would be organised, explain how you would persuade adults who have not studied for years to take part, and outline the longer-term benefits for the town.

bullets: how the festival would be organised
how you would attract reluctant learners
the longer-term benefits for the town

tasks.lead: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend Chris.

stimulus.body:
From: Chris
Subject: Too old to start again?

I've finally admitted that I can't stand my job, and I'm seriously thinking about retraining as a graphic designer. The catch: it means studying for two years while working part-time, and part of me thinks I'm too old to go back to being a student.

You changed direction yourself, didn't you? Be honest with me - is it worth it, and how did you cope?

Chris

bullets: your honest opinion on whether Chris should retrain
how Chris could cope with studying while working
encouragement based on your own or others' experience
